hypothesis: If a hybrid deep learning architecture combining a 2D CNN for surface encoding, a GRU for polymer sequence encoding, and a multi-head attention mechanism to model their interaction is trained on high-throughput MD-derived binding free energies, then it will predict polymer-surface adhesion more accurately than single-encoder baseline models, because the attention mechanism explicitly captures the heterogeneous, position-dependent complementarity between polymer sequence features and surface spatial patterns that governs binding affinity.
